params.name: rations
params.doc: livestock rations per feed alternative (cr per head)

params.name: lio
params.doc: land required per livestock feed alternative (ha per head)

params.name: llab
params.doc: labor for livestock feed (man-days per head)

params.name: crev
params.doc: crop revenue time series (cr per ha)

params.name: ravg
params.doc: average crop revenue = mean over ty of crev(c,ty)

params.name: prdev
params.doc: price deviations for crops (cr per ton)

params.name: pcost
params.doc: cropping technology cost = sum over km of techc(p,km)

params.name: a
params.doc: cropping constant (0 if activity grows cotton-h, else 1)

vars.name: xcrop
vars.doc: cropping activities (ha); ub xcropl where set

vars.name: revenue
vars.doc: revenue from crop and livestock sales (cr)

descriptions.intent: Total livestock raised equals the livestock supported across all feeding alternatives.
descriptions.structured: A single (non-indexed) equality: the scalar variable xlive (total livestock, head) equals the sum over feeding alternatives r of xliver[r] (head per feed technique).
descriptions.formal: Scalar equality. LHS = xlive. RHS = sum_{r in r} xliver[r]. Relation ==. No index conditions.

target_pyomo: model.lbal = Constraint(expr=model.xlive == sum(model.xliver[r] for r in model.r))

descriptions.intent: For each land type, the land used can't exceed the land available. Land is used by crops (only on land types that can grow crops), by land converted in from other types, and by livestock feeding.
descriptions.structured: Indexed over land type s. Left side has three terms: (a) ONLY when s is a crop-land (sc = good, medium): the sum over cropping activities p feasible on s of a[p]*xcrop[p,s]; (b) sum over source land types sp of ldp[s,sp]*lswitch[sp] (downgrading); (c) sum over feed alternatives r of lio[s,r]*xliver[r]. Relation <= available land landc[s].
descriptions.formal: For all s: ( [s in sc] * sum_{p:(p,s) in ps} a[p]*xcrop[p,s] ) + sum_{sp in s} ldp[s,sp]*lswitch[sp] + sum_{r in r} lio[s,r]*xliver[r] <= landc[s]. Note $ps(p,s) restricts the crop sum and $sc(s) zeroes the crop term on pasture.

target_pyomo:
def landb_rule(model, s):
    crop_term = 0
    if s in model.sc:
        crop_term = sum(model.a[p]*model.xcrop[p,s] for p in model.p if (p,s) in model.ps)
    downgrade_term = sum(model.ldp[s,sp]*model.lswitch[sp] for sp in model.s)
    livestock_term = sum(model.lio[s,r]*model.xliver[r] for r in model.r)
    return crop_term + downgrade_term + livestock_term <= model.landc[s]
model.landb = Constraint(model.s, rule=landb_rule)
target_math: landb(s).. sum(p$ps(p,s), a(p)*xcrop(p,s))$sc(s) + sum(sp, ldp(s,sp)*lswitch(sp)) + sum(r, lio(s,r)*xliver(r)) =l= landc(s)

descriptions.intent: Farm income equals sales revenue plus the value of the food the family keeps to eat, minus labor, ration, veterinary, and cropping costs, minus a risk penalty for year-to-year price swings.
descriptions.structured: Scalar equality defining yfarm: revenue + vsc*(sum over bundles dr of cons[dr]) - labcost - rationr - vetcost - cropcost - phi*(sum over years ty of pdev[ty]+ndev[ty]) / (number of years).
descriptions.formal: yfarm == revenue + vsc*sum_{dr} cons[dr] - labcost - rationr - vetcost - cropcost - phi*sum_{ty}(pdev[ty]+ndev[ty])/card(ty). card(ty)=10.

target_pyomo: model.income = Constraint(expr=model.yfarm == model.revenue + model.vsc*sum(model.cons[dr] for dr in model.dr) - model.labcost - model.rationr - model.vetcost - model.cropcost - model.phi*sum(model.pdev[ty]+model.ndev[ty] for ty in model.ty)/len(model.ty))
target_math: income.. yfarm =e= revenue + vsc*sum(dr, cons(dr)) - labcost - rationr - vetcost - cropcost - phi*sum(ty, pdev(ty)+ndev(ty))/card(ty)
